tools.description: WRITE-ONCE: do NOT call this tool with empty or placeholder arguments to probe or discover its schema — the required `body` field is listed in this schema; if you are not ready to post a real comment, call `noop` instead. Adds a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. Must be the final intended comment — not a placeholder or test value. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). {"number":1824,"title":"Fix marketplace --ref not propagated to relative plugin sources (#1811)","body":"## TL;DR

Propagate the marketplace's registered `--ref` to relative string plugin sources so `apm install plugin@marketplace` resolves against the registered branch, not the default branch.

## Problem (WHY)

When a marketplace is registered with `apm marketplace add owner/repo --ref feat/xxx`, and its `marketplace.json` uses relative string sources (e.g. `"./plugins/my-plugin"`), `apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace` downloads the plugin from the **default branch** instead of the registered `--ref`.

The ref is correctly persisted and used to fetch `marketplace.json`, but `resolve_marketplace_plugin` calls `resolve_plugin_source` without passing `source.ref`. The canonical string has no `#ref` suffix, so the downloader resolves against the default branch. The same gap exists in the GitLab structured-ref path where `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` receives `None` for string sources.

## Approach (WHAT)

Two surgical changes in `resolve_marketplace_plugin`:

1. **GitHub-family path:** After computing the canonical, append `#source.ref` when the plugin is a relative string source, no `#ref` is already present, no explicit `version_spec` overrides it, and `source.ref` is not `"main"` or `"HEAD"`.

2. **GitLab/non-GitHub structured path:** Fall back to `source.ref` in the ref argument to `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` when `path_ref` is `None` and no `version_spec`.

## Validation

- 5 new regression tests covering both GitHub and GitLab hosts (ref propagation, main exclusion, version_spec override)
- All 111 existing + new tests pass
- Lint (ruff check, ruff format, pylint R0801) clean

diff --git a/src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py b/src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py
index 6e0463a6d..bc0e24bfe 100644
--- a/src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py
+++ b/src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py
@@ -820,8 +820,16 @@ def _emit_warning(msg: str) -> None:
             plugin, plugin_root=manifest.plugin_root
         )
         if in_repo_path:
+            # Fall back to the marketplace's registered ref when the plugin
+            # source itself declares no ref and no version_spec overrides it.
+            # "main" / "HEAD" are excluded because they represent the default
+            # branch -- appending them would be a no-op at best and misleading
+            # when the repo's actual default branch has a different name.
+            effective_ref = version_spec or path_ref
+            if not effective_ref and source.ref and source.ref not in ("main", "HEAD"):
+                effective_ref = source.ref
             dep_ref = _gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference(
-                source, in_repo_path, version_spec or path_ref
+                source, in_repo_path, effective_ref
             )
             canonical = dep_ref.to_canonical()
 
@@ -858,6 +866,27 @@ def _emit_warning(msg: str) -> None:
         plugin, source, canonical, dep_ref
     )
 
+    # ---- Propagate marketplace registered ref (#1811) ----
+    # When a marketplace is registered with ``--ref feat/xxx`` and the plugin
+    # uses a relative string source (e.g. ``"./plugins/my-plugin"``), the
+    # canonical built by ``resolve_plugin_source`` carries no ``#ref`` suffix.
+    # Without this block the plugin would resolve against the default branch
+    # instead of the registered ref.
+    # "main" / "HEAD" are excluded to avoid appending a no-op suffix; if the
+    # repo's actual default branch is not named "main" and the user pinned
+    # ``--ref main``, this condition silently drops the ref -- fixing that
+    # would require knowing the repo's real default branch which is not
+    # available at this stage.
+    if (
+        dep_ref is None
+        and not version_spec
+        and isinstance(plugin.source, str)
+        and "#" not in canonical
+        and source.ref
+        and source.ref not in ("main", "HEAD")
+    ):
+        canonical = f"{canonical}#{source.ref}"
+
     # ---- Version spec override ----
     # When version_spec is provided it either triggers semver-aware tag
     # resolution (for range expressions like ~2.1.0) or a raw ref override
diff --git a/tests/unit/marketplace/test_marketplace_resolver.py b/tests/unit/marketplace/test_marketplace_resolver.py
index 252fa035f..2ca5e4f11 100644
--- a/tests/unit/marketplace/test_marketplace_resolver.py
+++ b/tests/unit/marketplace/test_marketplace_resolver.py
@@ -1432,3 +1432,104 @@ def test_fqdn_shorthand_without_git_path_misclassifies(self, monkeypatch):
         assert good.is_virtual is True
         assert good.repo_url == "epm-ease/ai-apm-registry"
         assert good.virtual_path == "agents/reverse-architect"
+
+
+class TestMarketplaceRefPropagation:
+    """Registered --ref must propagate to relative string plugin sources (#1811)."""
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _manifest_with_plugin(plugin: MarketplacePlugin) -> MarketplaceManifest:
+        return MarketplaceManifest(name="mkt", plugins=(plugin,), plugin_root="")
+
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.fetch_or_cache")
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.get_marketplace_by_name")
+    def test_github_relative_source_propagates_registered_ref(self, mock_get, mock_fetch):
+        """A GitHub marketplace registered with --ref feat/xxx appends #feat/xxx."""

{"number":1824,"title":"Fix marketplace --ref not propagated to relative plugin sources (#1811)","body":"## TL;DR

Propagate the marketplace's registered `--ref` to relative string plugin sources so `apm install plugin@marketplace` resolves against the registered branch, not the default branch.

## Problem (WHY)

When a marketplace is registered with `apm marketplace add owner/repo --ref feat/xxx`, and its `marketplace.json` uses relative string sources (e.g. `"./plugins/my-plugin"`), `apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace` downloads the plugin from the **default branch** instead of the registered `--ref`.

The ref is correctly persisted and used to fetch `marketplace.json`, but `resolve_marketplace_plugin` calls `resolve_plugin_source` without passing `source.ref`. The canonical string has no `#ref` suffix, so the downloader resolves against the default branch. The same gap exists in the GitLab structured-ref path where `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` receives `None` for string sources.

## Approach (WHAT)

Two surgical changes in `resolve_marketplace_plugin`:

1. **GitHub-family path:** After computing the canonical, append `#source.ref` when the plugin is a relative string source, no `#ref` is already present, no explicit `version_spec` overrides it, and `source.ref` is not `"main"` or `"HEAD"`.

2. **GitLab/non-GitHub structured path:** Fall back to `source.ref` in the ref argument to `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` when `path_ref` is `None` and no `version_spec`.

## Validation

- 5 new regression tests covering both GitHub and GitLab hosts (ref propagation, main exclusion, version_spec override)
- All 111 existing + new tests pass
- Lint (ruff check, ruff format, pylint R0801) clean

{"number":1824,"title":"Fix marketplace --ref not propagated to relative plugin sources (#1811)","body":"## TL;DR

Propagate the marketplace's registered `--ref` to relative string plugin sources so `apm install plugin@marketplace` resolves against the registered branch, not the default branch.

## Problem (WHY)

When a marketplace is registered with `apm marketplace add owner/repo --ref feat/xxx`, and its `marketplace.json` uses relative string sources (e.g. `"./plugins/my-plugin"`), `apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace` downloads the plugin from the **default branch** instead of the registered `--ref`.

The ref is correctly persisted and used to fetch `marketplace.json`, but `resolve_marketplace_plugin` calls `resolve_plugin_source` without passing `source.ref`. The canonical string has no `#ref` suffix, so the downloader resolves against the default branch. The same gap exists in the GitLab structured-ref path where `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` receives `None` for string sources.

## Approach (WHAT)

Two surgical changes in `resolve_marketplace_plugin`:

1. **GitHub-family path:** After computing the canonical, append `#source.ref` when the plugin is a relative string source, no `#ref` is already present, no explicit `version_spec` overrides it, and `source.ref` is not `"main"` or `"HEAD"`.

2. **GitLab/non-GitHub structured path:** Fall back to `source.ref` in the ref argument to `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` when `path_ref` is `None` and no `version_spec`.

## Validation

- 5 new regression tests covering both GitHub and GitLab hosts (ref propagation, main exclusion, version_spec override)
- All 111 existing + new tests pass
- Lint (ruff check, ruff format, pylint R0801) clean

+    # uses a relative string source (e.g. ``"./plugins/my-plugin"``), the

{"number":1824,"title":"Fix marketplace --ref not propagated to relative plugin sources (#1811)","body":"## TL;DR

Propagate the marketplace's registered `--ref` to relative string plugin sources so `apm install plugin@marketplace` resolves against the registered branch, not the default branch.

## Problem (WHY)

When a marketplace is registered with `apm marketplace add owner/repo --ref feat/xxx`, and its `marketplace.json` uses relative string sources (e.g. `"./plugins/my-plugin"`), `apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace` downloads the plugin from the **default branch** instead of the registered `--ref`.

The ref is correctly persisted and used to fetch `marketplace.json`, but `resolve_marketplace_plugin` calls `resolve_plugin_source` without passing `source.ref`. The canonical string has no `#ref` suffix, so the downloader resolves against the default branch. The same gap exists in the GitLab structured-ref path where `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` receives `None` for string sources.

## Approach (WHAT)

Two surgical changes in `resolve_marketplace_plugin`:

1. **GitHub-family path:** After computing the canonical, append `#source.ref` when the plugin is a relative string source, no `#ref` is already present, no explicit `version_spec` overrides it, and `source.ref` is not `"main"` or `"HEAD"`.

2. **GitLab/non-GitHub structured path:** Fall back to `source.ref` in the ref argument to `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` when `path_ref` is `None` and no `version_spec`.

## Validation

- 5 new regression tests covering both GitHub and GitLab hosts (ref propagation, main exclusion, version_spec override)
- All 111 existing + new tests pass
- Lint (ruff check, ruff format, pylint R0801) clean

diff --git a/src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py b/src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py
index 6e0463a6d..bc0e24bfe 100644
--- a/src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py
+++ b/src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py
@@ -820,8 +820,16 @@ def _emit_warning(msg: str) -> None:
             plugin, plugin_root=manifest.plugin_root
         )
         if in_repo_path:
+            # Fall back to the marketplace's registered ref when the plugin
+            # source itself declares no ref and no version_spec overrides it.
+            # "main" / "HEAD" are excluded because they represent the default
+            # branch -- appending them would be a no-op at best and misleading
+            # when the repo's actual default branch has a different name.
+            effective_ref = version_spec or path_ref
+            if not effective_ref and source.ref and source.ref not in ("main", "HEAD"):
+                effective_ref = source.ref
             dep_ref = _gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference(
-                source, in_repo_path, version_spec or path_ref
+                source, in_repo_path, effective_ref
             )
             canonical = dep_ref.to_canonical()
 
@@ -858,6 +866,27 @@ def _emit_warning(msg: str) -> None:
         plugin, source, canonical, dep_ref
     )
 
+    # ---- Propagate marketplace registered ref (#1811) ----
+    # When a marketplace is registered with ``--ref feat/xxx`` and the plugin
+    # uses a relative string source (e.g. ``"./plugins/my-plugin"``), the
+    # canonical built by ``resolve_plugin_source`` carries no ``#ref`` suffix.
+    # Without this block the plugin would resolve against the default branch
+    # instead of the registered ref.
+    # "main" / "HEAD" are excluded to avoid appending a no-op suffix; if the
+    # repo's actual default branch is not named "main" and the user pinned
+    # ``--ref main``, this condition silently drops the ref -- fixing that
+    # would require knowing the repo's real default branch which is not
+    # available at this stage.
+    if (
+        dep_ref is None
+        and not version_spec
+        and isinstance(plugin.source, str)
+        and "#" not in canonical
+        and source.ref
+        and source.ref not in ("main", "HEAD")
+    ):
+        canonical = f"{canonical}#{source.ref}"
+
     # ---- Version spec override ----
     # When version_spec is provided it either triggers semver-aware tag
     # resolution (for range expressions like ~2.1.0) or a raw ref override

+
+
+class TestMarketplaceRefPropagation:
+    """Registered --ref must propagate to relative string plugin sources (#1811)."""
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _manifest_with_plugin(plugin: MarketplacePlugin) -> MarketplaceManifest:
+        return MarketplaceManifest(name="mkt", plugins=(plugin,), plugin_root="")
+
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.fetch_or_cache")
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.get_marketplace_by_name")
+    def test_github_relative_source_propagates_registered_ref(self, mock_get, mock_fetch):
+        """A GitHub marketplace registered with --ref feat/xxx appends #feat/xxx."""
+        source = MarketplaceSource(
+            name="mkt",
+            owner="acme",
+            repo="catalog",
+            host="github.com",
+            ref="feat/my-feature",
+        )
+        plugin = MarketplacePlugin(name="my-plugin", source="./plugins/my-plugin")
+        mock_get.return_value = source
+        mock_fetch.return_value = self._manifest_with_plugin(plugin)
+
+        result = resolve_marketplace_plugin("my-plugin", "mkt")
+        assert result.canonical == "acme/catalog/plugins/my-plugin#feat/my-feature"
+        assert result.dependency_reference is None
+
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.fetch_or_cache")
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.get_marketplace_by_name")
+    def test_github_relative_source_main_ref_not_appended(self, mock_get, mock_fetch):
+        """ref='main' is the default -- do not append #main to the canonical."""
+        source = MarketplaceSource(
+            name="mkt",
+            owner="acme",
+            repo="catalog",
+            host="github.com",
+            ref="main",
+        )
+        plugin = MarketplacePlugin(name="my-plugin", source="./plugins/my-plugin")
+        mock_get.return_value = source
+        mock_fetch.return_value = self._manifest_with_plugin(plugin)
+
+        result = resolve_marketplace_plugin("my-plugin", "mkt")
+        assert result.canonical == "acme/catalog/plugins/my-plugin"
+
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.fetch_or_cache")
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.get_marketplace_by_name")
+    def test_version_spec_overrides_registered_ref(self, mock_get, mock_fetch):
+        """Explicit version_spec takes precedence over registered ref."""
+        source = MarketplaceSource(
+            name="mkt",
+            owner="acme",
+            repo="catalog",
+            host="github.com",
+            ref="feat/my-feature",
+        )
+        plugin = MarketplacePlugin(name="my-plugin", source="./plugins/my-plugin")
+        mock_get.return_value = source
+        mock_fetch.return_value = self._manifest_with_plugin(plugin)
+
+        result = resolve_marketplace_plugin("my-plugin", "mkt", version_spec="v2.0.0")
+        assert result.canonical == "acme/catalog/plugins/my-plugin#v2.0.0"
+
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.fetch_or_cache")
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.get_marketplace_by_name")
+    def test_gitlab_relative_source_propagates_registered_ref(self, mock_get, mock_fetch):
+        """A GitLab marketplace registered with --ref feat/xxx sets ref in dep_ref."""
+        source = MarketplaceSource(
+            name="mkt",
+            owner="acme",
+            repo="catalog",
+            host="gitlab.com",
+            ref="feat/my-feature",
+        )
+        plugin = MarketplacePlugin(name="my-plugin", source="registry/my-plugin")
+        mock_get.return_value = source
+        mock_fetch.return_value = self._manifest_with_plugin(plugin)
+
+        result = resolve_marketplace_plugin("my-plugin", "mkt")
+        assert result.dependency_reference is not None
+        assert result.dependency_reference.reference == "feat/my-feature"
+
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.fetch_or_cache")
+    @patch("apm_cli.marketplace.resolver.get_marketplace_by_name")
+    def test_gitlab_relative_source_main_ref_not_propagated(self, mock_get, mock_fetch):
+        """ref='main' should not be propagated to the GitLab dep_ref."""
+        source = MarketplaceSource(
+            name="mkt",
+            owner="acme",
+            repo="catalog",
+            host="gitlab.com",
+            ref="main",
+        )
+        plugin = MarketplacePlugin(name="my-plugin", source="registry/my-plugin")
+        mock_get.return_value = source
+        mock_fetch.return_value = self._manifest_with_plugin(plugin)
+
+        result = resolve_marketplace_plugin("my-plugin", "mkt")
+        assert result.dependency_reference is not None
+        assert result.dependency_reference.reference is None

{"number":1824,"title":"Fix marketplace --ref not propagated to relative plugin sources (#1811)","body":"## TL;DR

Propagate the marketplace's registered `--ref` to relative string plugin sources so `apm install plugin@marketplace` resolves against the registered branch, not the default branch.

## Problem (WHY)

When a marketplace is registered with `apm marketplace add owner/repo --ref feat/xxx`, and its `marketplace.json` uses relative string sources (e.g. `"./plugins/my-plugin"`), `apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace` downloads the plugin from the **default branch** instead of the registered `--ref`.

The ref is correctly persisted and used to fetch `marketplace.json`, but `resolve_marketplace_plugin` calls `resolve_plugin_source` without passing `source.ref`. The canonical string has no `#ref` suffix, so the downloader resolves against the default branch. The same gap exists in the GitLab structured-ref path where `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` receives `None` for string sources.

## Approach (WHAT)

Two surgical changes in `resolve_marketplace_plugin`:

1. **GitHub-family path:** After computing the canonical, append `#source.ref` when the plugin is a relative string source, no `#ref` is already present, no explicit `version_spec` overrides it, and `source.ref` is not `"main"` or `"HEAD"`.

2. **GitLab/non-GitHub structured path:** Fall back to `source.ref` in the ref argument to `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` when `path_ref` is `None` and no `version_spec`.

## Validation

- 5 new regression tests covering both GitHub and GitLab hosts (ref propagation, main exclusion, version_spec override)
- All 111 existing + new tests pass
- Lint (ruff check, ruff format, pylint R0801) clean

{"number":1811,"title":"[BUG] marketplace add --ref: registered ref not propagated to relative plugin source resolution","body":"**Describe the bug**

When a marketplace is registered with `apm marketplace add owner/repo --ref `, and its `marketplace.json` uses relative string sources (e.g. `\"./plugins/my-plugin\"`), `apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace` downloads the plugin from the repository's **default branch** rather than the registered `--ref` branch.

The ref is correctly persisted in `~/.apm/marketplaces.json` and correctly used to fetch `marketplace.json` (`client.py` passes `source.ref` to `fetch_or_cache`). However, in `resolver.py`, `resolve_marketplace_plugin` calls `_resolve_relative_source(source_str, source.owner, source.repo)` **without passing `source.ref`**. The canonical string (`owner/repo/plugins/my-plugin`) has no `#ref` suffix, so the downloader resolves against the default branch.

**To Reproduce**

1. Publish a repo with a `marketplace.json` on a non-default branch listing a plugin via a relative source. That plugin subdirectory does **not** exist on `main`:

   ```json
   {
     "name": "my-marketplace",
     "plugins": [{ "name": "my-plugin", "source": "./plugins/my-plugin" }]
   }
   ```

2. Register the marketplace pinned to that branch:

   ```
   apm marketplace add owner/catalog-repo --ref feat/my-feature --name my-marketplace
   ```

3. Install the plugin:

   ```
   apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace --target claude
   ```

4. Install resolves against the **default branch** (`main`), not `feat/my-feature`. Because the subdirectory only exists on `feat/my-feature`, the install fails:

   ```
   Error: Subdirectory not found: plugins/my-plugin
   ```

**Expected behavior**

`apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace` should install the plugin from `feat/my-feature` — the ref the marketplace was registered with.

**Root cause**

In `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py`, `resolve_marketplace_plugin` builds the canonical for a relative string source by calling:

```python
canonical = resolve_plugin_source(
    plugin,
    marketplace_owner=source.owner,
    marketplace_repo=source.repo,
    plugin_root=manifest.plugin_root,
)
```

`source.ref` is not passed. `_resolve_relative_source` returns `owner/repo/plugins/my-plugin` with **no `#ref` suffix**. The only code path that appends a ref to the canonical is the `version_spec` block at the end of the function, which fires only when the user explicitly passes a ref override via `plugin@mkt#ref` notation.

For GitLab / `*.ghe.com` marketplaces, `_source_needs_explicit_git_path` returns `True` and a structured `DependencyReference` is built (with an explicit `ref` when provided), so that host class is also affected when the dict source lacks its own `ref` field and no `version_spec` is supplied.

**Workaround**

Pass the ref explicitly at install time:

```
apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace#feat/my-feature
```

The `#feat/my-feature` becomes `version_spec` and is appended to the canonical.

**Proposed fix**

In `resolve_marketplace_plugin`, after computing `canonical` for an in-marketplace relative string source (and before the `version_spec` override block), propagate `source.ref` when no ref is already present and no explicit `version_spec` overrides it:

```python
# Propagate marketplace ref for intra-repo relative sources.
# Without this, a marketplace registered with --ref feat/xxx still downloads
# plugins against the default branch instead of the registered ref.
if (
    dep_ref is None
    and not version_spec
    and isinstance(plugin.source, str)
    and "#" not in canonical
    and source.ref
    and source.ref not in ("main", "HEAD")
):
    canonical = f"{canonical}#{source.ref}"
```

The existing test `TestGitHubMarketplace.test_github_marketplace_relative_source_returns_bare_canonical` (which uses `branch="main"`) continues to pass unchanged, since the condition excludes `"main"`.

**Environment**

- OS: Linux
- APM Version: 0.18.0 (also verified against 0.20.0 main — `source.ref` is unused in `resolve_marketplace_plugin` in the current upstream)

**Additional context**

The gap is easy to miss because the first half of the install flow _looks_ correct: `apm marketplace list` shows the right ref, and the `marketplace.json` is fetched from the right branch. The failure only surfaces later when the plugin's subdirectory is absent on the default branch.","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/microsoft/apm/issues/1811","user":{"login":"chkp-roniz","id":35386615,"profile_url":"https://github.com/chkp-roniz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35386615?v=4"},"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","labels":["priority/high","theme/portability","area/marketplace","type/bug","status/accepted","status/triaged"],"comments":1,"reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-06-16T12:17:02Z","updated_at":"2026-06-16T14:28:43Z"}

{"number":1824,"title":"Fix marketplace --ref not propagated to relative plugin sources (#1811)","body":"## TL;DR

Propagate the marketplace's registered `--ref` to relative string plugin sources so `apm install plugin@marketplace` resolves against the registered branch, not the default branch.

## Problem (WHY)

When a marketplace is registered with `apm marketplace add owner/repo --ref feat/xxx`, and its `marketplace.json` uses relative string sources (e.g. `"./plugins/my-plugin"`), `apm install my-plugin@my-marketplace` downloads the plugin from the **default branch** instead of the registered `--ref`.

The ref is correctly persisted and used to fetch `marketplace.json`, but `resolve_marketplace_plugin` calls `resolve_plugin_source` without passing `source.ref`. The canonical string has no `#ref` suffix, so the downloader resolves against the default branch. The same gap exists in the GitLab structured-ref path where `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` receives `None` for string sources.

## Approach (WHAT)

Two surgical changes in `resolve_marketplace_plugin`:

1. **GitHub-family path:** After computing the canonical, append `#source.ref` when the plugin is a relative string source, no `#ref` is already present, no explicit `version_spec` overrides it, and `source.ref` is not `"main"` or `"HEAD"`.

2. **GitLab/non-GitHub structured path:** Fall back to `source.ref` in the ref argument to `_gitlab_in_marketplace_dependency_reference` when `path_ref` is `None` and no `version_spec`.

## Validation

- 5 new regression tests covering both GitHub and GitLab hosts (ref propagation, main exclusion, version_spec override)
- All 111 existing + new tests pass
- Lint (ruff check, ruff format, pylint R0801) clean

## APM Review Panel: `ship_with_followups`

> Correct two-site fix for silent --ref loss in marketplace plugin installs; ship after CHANGELOG entry; HEAD exclusion regression trap and missing debug logs are the priority follow-ups.

The two changed lines restore the user promise that `apm install plugin@marketplace` with a registered `--ref` governs plugin installation for relative string sources, not just `marketplace.json` fetching. The five new unit tests are tight regression traps on both the GitHub canonical-append path and the GitLab effective_ref path; they are the strongest positive signal in this PR.

Three themes converge across five active panelists and constitute the complete actionable gap set. First, the CHANGELOG omission: oss-growth-hacker and doc-writer independently converge on the missing `[Unreleased] > ### Fixed` entry -- this is the one pre-merge ask, since contributor attribution and user self-triage both depend on it. Second, two silent canonical mutations carry no `logger.debug()` calls; the adjacent host-backfill block (lines 843-854) establishes the mandatory pattern -- every silent canonical mutation gets a debug trace -- and cli-logging-expert flags both absences as recommended; devx-ux-expert independently confirms the `--verbose` user-promise impact. Third, and highest-signal per test-evidence weighting rules: test-coverage-expert returns `outcome: missing` for the HEAD exclusion test on both new code blocks. A single tuple edit removing `'HEAD'` from either exclusion causes zero existing tests to fail yet `#HEAD` begins appearing in canonicals. This missing-test finding on a behavioral guard ranks above all opinion-only recommended findings and must be addressed in the same release cycle as the fix.

Supply-chain's `_validate_ref` recommendation is defense-in-depth: downstream subprocess arg-lists and URL encoding already neutralize crafted ref characters, and the threat vector requires direct edit of a local `marketplaces.json` file. The integration-floor gap (install canonical through lockfile round-trip) is real but appropriate as a follow-up issue given the tight resolver-boundary unit coverage already present. Doc updates to the `--ref` CLI reference and the `main`/`HEAD` silent-drop caveat are a genuine omission that devx-ux-expert and doc-writer agree on; they belong in the same or an adjacent PR but do not hold up the behavior fix itself.

**Dissent.** No material disagreements among panelists. Supply-chain-security (lockfile non-determinism angle) and devx-ux-expert (broken user promise angle) frame the `main`/`HEAD` silent-drop edge case through complementary lenses on the same pre-existing behavior -- both rate it nit-tier for this PR since the fix does not introduce or worsen the edge case.

**Aligned with:** **Portable by manifest** -- The fix directly advances portability: marketplace-registered `--ref` is now correctly propagated to relative plugin source canonicals, making installs reproducible across machines regardless of default-branch assumptions on the plugin repository. A lockfile written after this fix encodes the canonical with the explicit ref fragment, eliminating the silent drift that issue #1811 reported. **Secure by default** -- Downstream ref consumers (git subprocess arg-list, URL-encoded API calls) are safe by construction. Supply-chain-security correctly notes that `_validate_ref` -- the project's explicit ref-injection control, already called in `_fetch_git` and `_fetch_local` -- is absent at the new canonical assembly point; adding it is a low-effort defense-in-depth follow-up.

**Growth signal.** oss-growth-hacker flags this as the second non-trivial contribution from sergio-sisternes-epam; two substantive PRs from the same employer is a leading org-level adoption signal. <summary>Full per-persona findings</summary>

#### Python Architect

- **[recommended]** `*.ghe.com` marketplace + relative string source + registered ref combination is untested at `tests/unit/marketplace/test_marketplace_resolver.py:1437`

  *Suggested:* Add a sixth test: `MarketplaceSource(host='corp.ghe.com', ref='feat/xxx', ...)`, plugin `source='./plugins/p'` -> expect `result.canonical == 'corp.ghe.com/acme/catalog/plugins/p#feat/xxx'` and `result.dependency_reference is None`.
- **[nit]** Magic exclusion tuple `('main', 'HEAD')` duplicated at `resolver.py:829` and `resolver.py:886` at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:829`
  The same two-element literal appears independently in both the GitLab effective_ref block and the GitHub ref-propagation block. If the exclusion policy is extended, both sites must be updated in sync.
  *Suggested:* Add `_DEFAULT_REF_EXCLUSIONS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({'main', 'HEAD'})` module-level constant.
- **[nit]** Silent skip when `'#'` already in canonical produces no debug trace at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:884`
  *Suggested:* `logger.debug('Ref propagation skipped for %s@%s: canonical already carries fragment %r', plugin_name, marketplace_name, canonical)`
- **[nit]** Procedural pipeline, no new patterns introduced -- appropriate for two-site bug fix scope; no abstraction changes warranted.

#### CLI Logging Expert

- **[recommended]** GitHub ref-propagation block mutates canonical with no `logger.debug()` -- breaks established pattern set by adjacent host-backfill block at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:888`
  Lines 843-854 establish the pattern: every silent canonical mutation gets a `logger.debug()` call. In `--verbose` mode, a developer cannot determine whether the ref fragment came from the plugin source, `version_spec`, or the marketplace-registration fallback.
  *Suggested:* After line 888: `logger.debug("Propagated marketplace ref '%s' to canonical for %s@%s (#1811)", source.ref, plugin_name, marketplace_name)`
- **[recommended]** GitLab `effective_ref` fallback (line 830) has no `logger.debug()` call -- resolution decision is untraced at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:830`
  *Suggested:* Inside the `if not effective_ref...` branch: `logger.debug("Propagated marketplace ref '%s' to GitLab in-repo dep for %s@%s (#1811)", source.ref, plugin_name, marketplace_name)`
- **[nit]** Silent exclusion of `'main'`/`'HEAD'` refs emits nothing at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:886` -- a debug trace when exclusion fires would help agents diagnose the known edge case.

#### DevX UX Expert

- **[recommended]** Silent exclusion of `--ref main`/`HEAD` with no observable signal is invisible in a narrow but real scenario at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:886`
  A user who explicitly types `apm marketplace add acme/catalog --ref main` on a repo whose actual default branch is NOT `main` expects `main` to be pinned; instead the fix silently drops it with no debug log, no warning.
  *Suggested:* `logger.debug("ref '%s' for %s@%s matches default-branch exclusion list; not propagating to canonical", source.ref, plugin_name, marketplace_name)` when exclusion fires.
- **[recommended]** When propagated `source.ref` does not exist on the plugin repo, the downstream error has no marketplace context at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:888`
  User sees generic git "Reference not found" error with no mention of the marketplace or the recovery action (`apm marketplace add <SOURCE> --ref <valid-ref>`).
- **[recommended]** `--ref` docs say it governs `marketplace.json` fetching only; after this PR it also governs plugin installation -- the doc is now incomplete at `docs/src/content/docs/reference/cli/marketplace.md`
  *Suggested:* Extend the `--ref` row to document: "governs both the `marketplace.json` fetch and, for relative string plugin sources, the ref used when installing plugins. `main`/`HEAD` are treated as the default branch and not propagated."
- **[nit]** HEAD exclusion asserted in code at two sites but has zero test coverage at `tests/unit/marketplace/test_marketplace_resolver.py:1465` -- parametrize `'main'`/`'HEAD'` in the main-exclusion tests.

#### Supply Chain Security Expert

- **[recommended]** `_validate_ref` is not called before `source.ref` is appended to the canonical string at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:888`
  `_validate_ref` (enforces `^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._/-]*$`) is called in `_fetch_git` and `_fetch_local` but not in `_fetch_github` or `_fetch_gitlab`. A crafted local `marketplaces.json` entry could embed characters that flow silently into the canonical. Downstream subprocess arg-lists and URL encoding mitigate in practice, but the explicit guard the project relies on is absent at the assembly point.
  *Suggested:* Call `_validate_ref(source.ref, marketplace_name)` before line 888 and before the GitLab `effective_ref` assignment at line 830.
- **[nit]** The `'main'`/`'HEAD'` exclusion silently drops explicitly user-pinned refs at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:886` -- two machines with different repo defaults install different commits from the same lockfile entry.
- **[nit]** GitLab `effective_ref` block lacks the explanatory comment present in the GitHub-family block at `src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py:829` -- copy rationale + known-limitation disclosure from lines 875-879.

#### OSS Growth Hacker

- **[recommended]** No CHANGELOG entry for this fix -- affected users cannot self-triage whether to upgrade at `CHANGELOG.md`
  Every other community fix in `[Unreleased] Fixed` has a CHANGELOG bullet with PR/issue refs and contributor attribution. Without the entry, the contributor gets no visible credit and release posts cannot cite the fix.
  *Suggested:* Add to `[Unreleased] > ### Fixed`: "`apm install plugin@marketplace` now resolves relative string plugin sources against the marketplace's registered `--ref` instead of silently falling back to the default branch. (by @sergio-sisternes-epam; closes #1811) (#1824)"
- **[nit]** Docs for `--ref` flag and install guide silently omit propagation guarantee now provided by this fix at `docs/src/content/docs/consumer/installing-from-marketplaces.md`.
- **[nit]** Known limitation (`--ref main` silently dropped when repo default branch differs) is buried in source comment, not surfaced to users.

#### Auth Expert -- inactive

Both changed hunks only touch the ref/fragment portion of canonical URLs and the `DependencyReference.reference` field. `AuthResolver` dispatches exclusively on host, port, and host_type -- none of which are modified. The existing `*.ghe.com` host-backfill auth block (#1285, lines 843-854) is unaffected and runs before the new ref-propagation block.

#### Doc Writer

- **[recommended]** Missing CHANGELOG entry under `[Unreleased] > Fixed` for the #1811 regression at `CHANGELOG.md`
  The fix corrects a silent misbehavior users were working around. Users upgrading who were affected have no changelog signal that it is resolved.
  *Suggested:* Add under `[Unreleased] > ### Fixed`: "`apm install plugin@marketplace` now correctly uses the marketplace's registered `--ref` when the plugin source is a relative string. Previously the ref was silently dropped, causing install to resolve against the default branch. (closes #1811) (#1824)"
- **[recommended]** `--ref` flag description does not document propagation to relative plugin sources or the `main`/`HEAD` exclusion caveat at `docs/src/content/docs/reference/cli/marketplace.md`
  *Suggested:* Extend the `--ref` row to clarify that the ref is also propagated at install time for relative string sources, and note that `main`/`HEAD` are excluded.
- **[nit]** Consumer install doc covers `--ref` for HTTPS URLs but not for relative plugin source propagation at `docs/src/content/docs/consumer/installing-from-marketplaces.md`.

#### Test Coverage Expert

- **[recommended]** HEAD exclusion from ref propagation is untested in both new code blocks at `tests/unit/marketplace/test_marketplace_resolver.py`
  Both new blocks gate on `source.ref not in ('main', 'HEAD')` but the new test class only covers `ref='main'`. Removing `'HEAD'` from either exclusion tuple causes zero test failures. Confirmed absence via grep.
  *Proof (missing):* `tests/unit/marketplace/test_marketplace_resolver.py::test_github_relative_source_head_ref_not_appended` -- proves: A marketplace registered with `--ref HEAD` does not append `#HEAD` to the canonical [devx, portability-by-manifest]
  `assert result.canonical == "acme/catalog/plugins/my-plugin"  # no #HEAD suffix`
  *Suggested:* Add tests for HEAD exclusion for both GitHub and GitLab paths (or parametrize existing main-exclusion tests with `('main', 'HEAD')`).
- **[recommended]** No integration-with-fixtures test walks `apm install plugin@marketplace` through the lockfile when marketplace carries a non-default `--ref` at `tests/integration/test_install_marketplace_phase4w3.py`
  The headline user promise is "install resolves against the registered branch." 5 unit tests defend the resolver in isolation, but no integration test traces the corrected canonical through install pipeline, lockfile write, and round-trip. Confirmed absence via grep on `tests/integration/`.
  *Proof (missing):* `tests/integration/test_install_marketplace_phase4w3.py::test_marketplace_install_propagates_registered_ref_to_lockfile` -- proves: `apm install plugin@marketplace` with registered `--ref feat/xxx` locks canonical as `owner/repo/path#feat/xxx` [portability-by-manifest, devx]
  `assert locked_entry["canonical"].endswith("#feat/my-feature")`
  *Suggested:* Add hermetic integration test driving install with fixture marketplace at `--ref feat/xxx` and asserting lockfile canonical ends with `#feat/xxx`.

#### Performance Expert -- inactive

The two changed files (`src/apm_cli/marketplace/resolver.py` and its unit-test companion) are not in the perf fast-path (cache/deps/install phases/pipeline/resolve); all 30 added lines are pre-download metadata computation -- boolean guards and f-string concatenation -- with zero impact on resolve round-trips, fetch bytes, materialization syscalls, or install wall-time.

</details>

<sub>This panel is advisory. It does not block merge. Re-apply the
`panel-review` label after addressing feedback to re-run.</sub>

5. **[DevX UX Expert + Doc Writer]** Update the `--ref` CLI reference doc to document propagation to relative plugin sources and the `main`/`HEAD` silent-drop caveat -- Post-fix, `--ref` semantics expanded silently beyond what the CLI reference documents. The `main`/`HEAD` exclusion edge case -- explicit `--ref main` silently dropped when the plugin repo's actual default branch differs -- needs a callout to avoid user confusion.

### Recommendation

Merge after the CHANGELOG entry is added (followup #1 -- the single pre-merge ask). The fix is correct, minimal, and safe; all panelists agree and the unit regression tests are strong. The HEAD exclusion regression trap (followup #2) and missing debug logs (followup #3) are the highest-signal post-merge items and should land in the same release cycle. `_validate_ref` defense-in-depth (followup #4) and the doc update (followup #5) can follow in adjacent PRs. The behavior fix is ready to ship once the CHANGELOG is in place.
